lesson.menu_desc: Opinions: I think / In my opinion + agreeing and disagreeing politely -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Agreeing & Disagreeing -- Opinions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Aline Sberci | Aula 15 -- Agreeing & Disagreeing

listenings.text: Hi Aline, this is Tom. Before our meeting, I want to share my opinion about the new project plan. In my opinion, we should launch the campaign in March, not in April. I think March gives us more time before the summer. I know some people disagree, and that is a fair point. They prefer April because the budget is ready then. But personally, I believe an earlier launch is better for the team. I would love to hear your point of view in the meeting. If you agree with me, great. If you disagree, that is fine too. We can find a solution together. Talk soon.

This is a quick voice note before the team meeting. I need to share my opinion clearly and politely. First, I agree with Tom that an early launch is a good idea. In my opinion, March works well for the marketing team. However, I disagree about the budget. I think we should wait two weeks to be sure. I want to suggest a small change, not a big one. I will say, I see your point, but I prefer a safer plan. I know it depends on the final numbers. My goal is to give my point of view, listen to the others, and find a fair solution that works for everyone.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom shares his opinion that the campaign should launch in March. Then Aline agrees that an early launch is a good idea. After that, she disagrees about the budget and explains why. Next, she suggests waiting two weeks to be sure. Finally, the team finds a fair solution that works for everyone.
